introSection: Runaway Mine Train, is a steel hybrid roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas, USA. Built by Arrow Development, the attraction opened on July 23, 1966 and is the oldest roller coaster in the park. In 1996, the ride was renamed to Mine Train to prevent confusion with Runaway Mountain. In September 2006, Runaway Mine Train was designated an ACE Coaster Landmark by the American Coaster Enthusiasts.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened as Run-A-Way Mine Train in 1966.

The ride's name was shortened to just Mine Train in 1996. It was later changed to Runaway Mine Train.

The ride's height requirement was raised from to  in 2025.

fullText:
Runaway Mine Train, is a steel hybrid roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas, USA. Built by Arrow Development, the attraction opened on July 23, 1966 and is the oldest roller coaster in the park. In 1996, the ride was renamed to Mine Train to prevent confusion with Runaway Mountain. In September 2006, Runaway Mine Train was designated an ACE Coaster Landmark by the American Coaster Enthusiasts.

The ride opened as Run-A-Way Mine Train in 1966.

The ride's name was shortened to just Mine Train in 1996. It was later changed to Runaway Mine Train.

The ride's height requirement was raised from to  in 2025.



Brown track and unpainted supports.
